design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate island terrain while defeating enemies with rifle; collect power-ups to transform into Megagon for temporary power surge to overcome obstacles
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammo management as human form) vs. temporary invulnerability (Megagon transformation with limited hit points based on score)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Stranded Marine survivor fighting through a monster-infested island to reach rescue; transformation into superhuman form as fantasy payoff
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Asymmetrical dual-form combat system where weak rifle-using baseline form contrasts sharply with powerful but fragile Megagon form; design forces deliberate form-switching strategy rather than relying on single playstyle

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system typical of NES platformers; death resets player to checkpoint
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can choose when to activate Megagon transformation via Mega-Key collection, allowing some control over difficulty moments; however transformation fragility (1 HP per 5000 points scored) creates high-risk/high-reward decision-making

progression.upgrade_system: Mega-Key power-up grants temporary Megagon transformation; transformation grants variable hit points based on accumulated score (1 HP per 5000 points), creating score-based scaling progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear progression across island; Mega-Key must be collected to access transformation ability
replayability_hooks: Score accumulation affects Megagon durability (higher scores = more HP when transformed)
Varied enemy types require different combat strategies
Boss encounters at end stages provide skill-check moments

world_building_techniques: Island setting as survival scenario (plane crash stranding setup)
Japanese version provides alternative narrative layer (scientist and 'Macho Max' drug on Monster Island)

boss_design.philosophy: Major skill gates at stage endings; final boss requires mastery of Megagon transformation and resource management
notable_bosses: Final boss: appears at end of island crossing (documented in screenshot context)

danger_signals: Monster-infested island environment signals hostile territory
Variety of enemies indicates escalating threat density
environmental_storytelling: Island setting establishes isolation and survival context
Enemy types populate Monster Island ecosystem
